name: Maritime documents and border gates
description: Core maritime documentation and border-readiness gates for Cortex SEA mode. Covers global sea operational documents, SOLAS VGM, maritime DG documentation, US ISF, EU ICS2/ENS, and general customs document readiness.
source_strategy: Official regulatory/customs sources for VGM, ISF, ICS2/ENS, and IMDG concepts; authored operational rules for general B/L, shipping instructions, invoice, packing list, certificate of origin, and permits.

sources.title: IMO - Verification of the gross mass of a packed container
U.S. Customs and Border Protection - Import Security Filing (ISF) when to submit
European Commission - ICS2 live for maritime and inland waterway transport
IMO - International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code
UK GOV - Dangerous goods form reproduced from IMDG Code Chapter 5.4

known_gaps: No licensed full IMDG document/rule set

known_limitations: No licensed full IMDG document/rule set

confidence_policy.verified: Directly supported by official regulatory/customs source.
confidence_policy.verified_concept: Concept supported by official source, but exact form or implementation can vary.
confidence_policy.authored: Cortex operational rule authored from common logistics practice; not a universal statute.
confidence_policy.estimated: Used only for planning/cutoff approximations; live validation required.

integration_notes: SEA-F links to SEA-A: if cargo is dangerous goods, require IMO_DGD and CPC when containerized.
SEA-F links to SEA-B: if containerized, require VGM; if reefer or special equipment, document/cutoff validation should be handled by SEA-I/booking layer.
SEA-F links to SEA-I: carrier/terminal document cutoffs and sailing-specific deadlines belong to SEA-I.
SEA-F should output readiness blockers, not legal advice.
